articles.title: Wealthsimple Joins Prediction Markets Fray as Canada Opens Up
articles.summary: Wealthsimple, Canada's largest robo-advisor with over $15B in assets, is launching prediction market offerings as Canadian regulators loosen restrictions on the sector. The move signals prediction markets are going mainstream beyond crypto-native platforms, potentially bringing millions of retail investors into political and event betting for the first time.

articles.title: Opinion | Trump is right about polls — and the better bet
articles.summary: Trump's calling out polling accuracy again, and this time someone thinks he's onto something about systematic polling errors or methodology flaws. If polls are consistently missing the mark, smart money will keep flowing toward betting markets as the more reliable gauge of actual electoral sentiment.

articles.title: Predicting the Highest Seed to Qualify for the Final Four According to Kalshi
articles.summary: Kalshi traders are betting on which tournament underdog will make the deepest run, with markets open on the highest seed number that reaches the Final Four. The action reveals where smart money thinks the biggest March Madness upset value lies, giving bettors a read on which long shots the crowd actually believes in versus the ones getting hyped for no reason.

articles.title: The Scrutiny of Prediction Markets Has Stepped Up in Washington—and Beyond. What's Next?
articles.summary: Washington regulators are cranking up their oversight of prediction markets, scrutinizing everything from election betting to regulatory compliance. The increased attention could reshape which platforms operate in the US and how Americans access political betting markets.

articles.title: How Prediction Markets Are Changing the Music Industry
articles.summary: Music labels and streaming platforms are starting to use prediction markets to forecast which songs will hit, which artists will break through, and how album releases will perform. Smart money is realizing that fan sentiment and crowd wisdom often beat A&R executives at spotting the next big thing, creating new opportunities for bettors who understand music trends better than industry insiders.

articles.title: A decade after Nevada fought daily fantasy companies, it's deja vu with Kalshi
articles.summary: Nevada is battling Kalshi over prediction markets the same way it fought DraftKings and FanDuel over daily fantasy sports a decade ago. The state's regulatory stance could shape how prediction markets operate nationwide, especially as other jurisdictions watch Nevada's moves on event contracts and gaming licenses.

articles.title: Exclusive: Trader made nearly $1 million on Polymarket with remarkably accurate Iran bets
articles.summary: A Polymarket trader turned geopolitical analysis into serious cash, banking nearly $1M by correctly predicting Iran-related events with unusual precision. This shows how deep regional expertise can create massive edges in political prediction markets, especially when most bettors rely on headlines instead of real intelligence.

articles.title: Lawmakers Introduce Bipartisan Bill Banning Sports Bets on Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Bipartisan lawmakers want to ban sports betting on prediction markets, targeting platforms that let users wager on game outcomes alongside political and economic events. The move would force a cleaner separation between traditional sportsbooks and prediction markets, potentially protecting the latter from sports betting's regulatory headaches while limiting their revenue streams.

articles.title: Polymarket’s Coming-Out Party in Washington Was a Disaster
articles.summary: Polymarket's first major Washington DC event to court regulators and legitimize prediction markets went sideways, likely featuring awkward exchanges between crypto-betting executives and skeptical policymakers. The fumbled debut signals that regulatory acceptance remains elusive, which could keep institutional money on the sidelines and force serious bettors to stick with overseas platforms.

articles.title: Illinois Regulators Say Prediction Markets Are Illegal Gambling, But Bettors — And The Trump Family — Love Them
articles.summary: Illinois regulators just declared prediction markets illegal gambling, setting up a direct clash with the booming industry that's attracted everyone from retail bettors to the Trump family. This signals a broader regulatory crackdown that could fragment the US market and force platforms to navigate a patchwork of state-by-state rules.

articles.title: Opinion | We Shouldn’t Want a World Run by Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Some writer thinks prediction markets running the world would be a dystopian nightmare and wants to pump the brakes on our beautiful, efficient future. The piece probably rehashes tired concerns about reducing human complexity to binary outcomes, which matters because these arguments could slow mainstream adoption just when we're hitting our stride.
